task_completion.description: Verify that the agent applied phase cancellation to remove center-panned lead vocals from the audio tracks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands (e.g., ffmpeg) using audio filters for phase cancellation/channel subtraction (like 'pan=stereo|c0=c0-c1|c1=c1-c0', 'stereotools', or 'extrastereo'), or an audio editor (like Audacity) showing channel splitting and inversion.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent overlaid the ktv_logo.png watermark in the bottom-right corner of the videos.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for ffmpeg commands using the 'overlay' filter with parameters placing the image at the bottom right (e.g., 'W-w:H-h', 'main_w-overlay_w:main_h-overlay_h', or specific pixel coordinates), or a video editor GUI showing the logo positioned in the bottom-right.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent embedded the .srt lyric files as selectable subtitle streams in the MKV container.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for ffmpeg commands that include the .srt file as an input ('-i track1.srt') and map it to a subtitle stream (e.g., '-c:s srt', '-c:s copy', or '-scodec copy'), or usage of a muxing GUI like MKVToolNix.

task_completion.description: Verify that the three processed MKV files were saved to the correct destination directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal 'ls' output or a file manager window showing 'track1_karaoke.mkv', 'track2_karaoke.mkv', and 'track3_karaoke.mkv' successfully created in '/home/ga/Videos/ktv_ready/'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the catalog.json file was created with the correct schema and venue name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal output (e.g., 'cat catalog.json') showing a JSON object with 'venue': 'Star KTV' and a 'processed_tracks' array containing the required metadata for all three tracks.

integrity.description: Ensure the media files were actually processed and not just renamed or created as empty files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for execution of genuine media processing tools (ffmpeg, Audacity, MKVToolNix) or scripts containing these commands, rather than simple 'mv', 'cp', or 'touch' commands masquerading as MKV files.

integrity.description: Ensure subtitles were embedded as selectable streams, not burned (hardcoded) into the video.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent did not use the 'subtitles=' video filter in ffmpeg (which burns the text into the video frames), but rather muxed the .srt file as a separate stream.

integrity.description: Ensure the JSON catalog contains entries for all three tracks, not just a copy-paste of the single example from the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the 'processed_tracks' array in catalog.json actually contains distinct entries for track1, track2, and track3, rather than just repeating track1 or only containing one item.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original tracks are synthetically generated. Track 1 has a center frequency of 1000Hz, Track 2 has 1500Hz, and Track 3 has 1200Hz. Phase cancellation should target these center-panned frequencies. The side frequencies (Track 1: 400/800Hz, Track 2: 500/900Hz, Track 3: 300/700Hz) should remain intact.
